radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with severe AS  // Assessment of aortic
calcifications no IV contrast

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  239 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  No comparison.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  The lymph nodes in the mediastinum (3, 14) are
increased in number but not in size.

Severe calcification of the aortic arch, moderate calcification of the
ascending and of the descending aorta.  At the level of the main pulmonary
artery (3, 29) the ascending aorta has a diameter of 32 x 33 mm, at the same
anatomical level, the descending aorta has a diameter of 25 x 28 mm.  The
diameter of the pulmonary artery is normal.  Severe aortic valve
calcifications.  Severe coronary calcifications, global enlargement of the
heart, no pericardial effusions.

No osteolytic lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral
bodies.  Mild degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression
fractures.  Moderate to severe upper lobe predominant centrilobular pulmonary
emphysema.  Signs of moderate chronic airways disease.  Moderate bilateral
pleural effusions with adjacent compressive atelectasis.  Focal atelectasis at
the bases of the lingular.  Several non suspicious small pulmonary nodules are
visualized (5, 196).  No evidence of suspicious lung lesions.  Moderate
respiratory motion at the bases of the lungs.

IMPRESSION: 

Aortic ___ are reported above.  Moderate bilateral pleural effusions
with moderate chronic airways disease.  Basal areas of atelectasis.  No
evidence of acute infectious or neoplastic changes.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with CHF with dobutamine requirement.  //
Transplant Evaluation.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast.

DLP:  ___

COMPARISON:  Prior CT scan from ___ (OSH).

FINDINGS: 

A right PICC line is identified with the distal tip in the SVC. A ___-___
catheter is identified with the distal tip in the right lower lobe pulmonary
artery. An AICD device is identified with the distal tip in the right
ventricle. There is severe cardiomegaly, with likely four-chamber enlargement.
No pericardial or pleural effusions are identified. Dense calcification of the
coronary arteries is noted. All no axillary, all supraclavicular, mediastinal
or hilar lymphadenopathy. Mild atheromatous calcification of the thoracic
aorta is noted.

Moderate centrilobular emphysema is identified, predominately within the upper
lobes. No evidence of interstitial pulmonary edema. No suspicious lung nodules
are identified. A calcified granuloma is identified in the right lower lobe
(603b:29).

Subdiaphragmatic structures are suboptimally assessed in the absence of
intravenous contrast, however the liver demonstrates slightly nodular contour.
Thickening of the adrenal glands is noted bilaterally, likely in keeping with
adrenal hyperplasia. Small amount of fluid/stranding is identified surrounding
the pancreatic head near the porta hepatis (2:70), not completely assessed.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:

Mild mole multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are evident.
No focal osteolytic or osteoblastic lesions are seen.

IMPRESSION:


1. Right ___ catheter identified with the distal tip in the right lower
lobe pulmonary artery. This needs to be retracted back to the level of the
right ventricular outflow tract.
2. Severe cardiomegaly with four-chamber enlargement. AICD device identified.
3. Dense coronary artery calcifications.
4. Moderate centrilobular emphysema predominate within the upper lobes.
5. Slightly nodular contour of the liver, suggestive of cirrhosis.
6. Thickening of adrenal glands bilaterally, likely in keeping with adrenal
hyperplasia.
7. Small amount of fluid/ stranding identified surrounding the pancreatic head
near the porta hepatis, not completely assess, likely related to trace ascites
rather than pancreatitis in the absence of clinical history of abdominal pain.

NOTIFICATION:  This consultation was reviewed with Dr. ___ by Dr. ___
telephone at 20:30 hr on ___, 25 min following discovery.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Lung cancer.  Sternal mass.

COMPARISON:  CT of the chest performed at an outside institution ___.

PROCEDURE:  CT-guided biopsies of soft tissue mass centered at the anterior
aspect of the manubrium/upper sternum.

After reviewing the patient's previous imaging studies and obtaining informed
consent, the patient was brought to the CT suite.  Initial axial non-contrast
images through the manubrium and upper sternum were obtained.  A timeout was
performed.  

Conscious sedation was provided by a radiology nurse trained in conscious
sedation including a total dosage of 50 mcg of fentanyl and 1.0 mg of
intravenous Versed.  The total intraservice time was 15 minutes during which
the patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously monitored.

The skin overlying the manubrium and upper sternum was prepped with Betadine
solution.  1% lidocaine was injected into the subcutaneous tissues for local
anesthetic.  A 16-gauge Achieve soft tissue biopsy device was advanced into
the lesion and four samples were taken from slightly different positions
within the lesion.  Specimens were taken to surgical pathology.

FINDINGS:

Similar to the previous CT are multiple pulmonary nodules and masses, the
largest in the superior segment of the left upper lobe measures approximately
3 cm (AP) x 2.3 cm (TV).  There is a small amount of right pleural fluid.  

There is atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta and there are
heavy coronary artery calcifications.

Again seen centered at the anterior aspect of the manubrium and the upper
sternum is a 5.9 cm (TV) x 3.5 cm (AP) destructive soft tissue mass.  There
are large areas of erosion and soft tissue infiltration of the medial left
clavicle at the clavicular manubrial junction.  Sternal wires are again seen
in the more inferior aspect of the sternum.

The patient tolerated the procedure well and was observed in the department
for two hours following the procedure.

The procedure was performed with and supervised by Dr. ___.

IMPRESSION:

Successful CT-guided biopsies of the soft tissue mass at the anterior aspect
of the manubrium/upper sternum.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Pedestrian truck by car, now with chest pain and chest wall
tenderness.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available due to patient's critical status.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest
following the uneventful administration of 75 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were generated and
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The imaged portion of the thyroid gland is unremarkable.  No
pathologically enlarged axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes are seen. 
There is no mediastinal hemorrhage or pneumomediastinum.  The thoracic aorta
is normal in caliber without evidence of acute aortic syndrome. 
Mild-to-moderate calcification of the aortic arch and descending thoracic
aorta is noted.  The pulmonary arterial trunk is normal in caliber.  The heart
is normal in size without pericardial effusion.  Mild-to-moderate global
coronary artery calcifications are present.  The esophagus is unremarkable.

The central tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental levels
bilaterally.  Posterior dependent positional changes are noted throughout the
lungs.  No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is present. 
Paraseptal emphysema is noted in the bilateral upper lobes.  No suspicious
pulmonary nodules or masses are identified.

Although this study is not tailored for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
contents, the imaged right upper abdomen demonstrates nodular contours in the
liver suggestive of underlying cirrhosis.  An accessory spleen is incidentally
noted in the left upper quadrant.  No acute abnormality is detected.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No acute fracture or hematoma is identified in the
thorax.  There is no osseous destructive lesion concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No acute fracture or evidence of solid organ injury in the thorax.
2.  Paraseptal emphysema in the upper lobes.
3.  Mild-to-moderate global coronary artery calcifications.
4.  Nodular-appearing liver suggestive of underlying cirrhosis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with cough, SOB// eval for interstitial disease

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.6 s, 34.5 cm; CTDIvol = 18.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 634.6
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 1.2 s, 7.8 cm; CTDIvol = 18.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 145.3
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 780 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest x-rays.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is unremarkable.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall mild atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  Mild
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta and aortic
valve.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
Bilateral large pleural effusions.  Small calcified pleural plaques are noted,
for example in the right lung base (4:192).

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall
thickening, bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Partial compressive atelectasis
of both lower lobes.  Left paramediastinal consolidations with punctate
indwelling calcifications in the left upper lobe.  No suspicious large lung
nodules or masses.

CHEST CAGE:
Moderate dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show several small calcified
gallstones.  Bilateral renal simple cysts measuring up to 6.5 cm.

IMPRESSION: 

Linear atelectasis in the left upper lobe.  Large bilateral pleural effusions
with subsequent compressive atelectasis of both lower lobes.
No evidence of interstitial lung disease.

axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

sections.findings:
NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is unremarkable.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall mild atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  Mild
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta and aortic
valve.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
Bilateral large pleural effusions.  Small calcified pleural plaques are noted,
for example in the right lung base (4:192).

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall
thickening, bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Partial compressive atelectasis
of both lower lobes.  Left paramediastinal consolidations with punctate
indwelling calcifications in the left upper lobe.  No suspicious large lung
nodules or masses.

CHEST CAGE:
Moderate dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show several small calcified
gallstones.  Bilateral renal simple cysts measuring up to 6.5 cm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with localized pancreatic cancer  // r/o
metastases

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS COORDINATED WITH
INTRAVENOUS INFUSION OF NONIONIC CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5
AND 1.25 MM THICK AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP
AXIAL IMAGES. SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS AND THE TOTAL
DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP reported separatelymGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Heterogeneously enlarged thyroid gland is similar to prior CT and not fully
characterized by this modality.

There are no enlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.  Heart size is normal, and no
pericardial effusion is present.  Previously present small bilateral pleural
effusions have resolved.

No new suspicious lytic or blastic skeletal lesions are evident.

Within the lungs, linear atelectasis is noted at both lung bases.  No new or
growing pulmonary nodules are detected.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No CT evidence of pulmonary metastases.

2.  Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for complete
description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with metastatic pancreatic cancer.  Restaging
study.

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, reconstructed as
contiguous 5 mm and 1.0 or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and
parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images. Sequential scanning of the abdomen
and pelvis  will be reported separately. Images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.1 s, 24.6 cm; CTDIvol = 8.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 195.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 7.8 s, 61.6 cm; CTDIvol = 10.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 673.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 870 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Multinodular appearance of the
enlarged thyroid (goiter) appears similar compared to prior imaging.  No
infiltration of surrounding structures.  No encasement of the trachea. No
supraclavicular adenopathy. No axillary adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions. 
Left-sided prepectoral Port-A-Cath in situ with the tip at the cavoatrial
junction.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Will be reported separately.

MEDIASTINUM: No mediastinal adenopathy.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Trace pericardial fluid.  Mild cardiomegaly.  No aortic
valve calcification.  No coronary artery calcification.  No aneurysmal
dilatation of the ascending aorta.  Mild atherosclerotic changes of the aortic
arch
PLEURA: No pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Millimetric pulmonary nodule in the left lower lobe is
unchanged.  No new or enlarging pulmonary nodules or masses.  Mild linear
atelectasis in the right lung base.  No confluent airspace consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  No
bronchiectasis.
3. VESSELS:  Mild dilatation of the pulmonary artery measuring 32 mm in
diameter and pulmonary hypertension should be excluded.
CHEST CAGE: No suspicious bony lesions.

IMPRESSION: 

No new or concerning pulmonary nodules or mediastinal adenopathy. No findings
to suggest intrathoracic metastatic disease.

Mild dilatation of the pulmonary artery and pulmonary hypertension should be
excluded.

For abdominal findings please refer to CT abdomen report.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with metastatic pancreatic cancer,
chemotherapy, now presenting for restaging.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 540 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  Chest CT dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber and mildly calcified.  Mild dilation
of the main pulmonary artery to 32 mm is unchanged and can be seen as sequelae
of chronic pulmonary hypertension.  No pericardial effusion.  A left
Port-A-Cath tip ends in the distal SVC.

No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  A 7 mm
lymph node at the thoracic inlet and is 8 mm subcarinal node are unchanged. 
22 x 11 mm hypodense ovoid structure anterior to the esophagus below the
subcarinal station is stable from most recent prior but new from ___,
potentially fluid in a pericardial recess (series 8, image 50; series 12,
image 64).

No new or growing pulmonary nodules.  No focal opacities concerning for focal
pneumonia. The airways are patent to at least the subsegmental level.  No
pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

The thyroid is enlarged and heterogeneous with multiple nodules, some
calcified, incompletely evaluated on this exam is similar the prior exam.

No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy in the chest cage.  Multilevel
degenerative changes are mild.

Please refer to the dedicated CT abdomen and pelvis report from the same day
for a description of sub-diaphragm findings.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No new or growing pulmonary nodules.
2.  Subcarinal lymph node appears slightly larger.  Close attention on
follow-up imaging.
3.  22 x 11 mm hypodense ovoid structure below the subcarinal station is
unchanged from prior but new from ___, could be fluid in a pericardial
recess.  Close attention on follow-up imaging.
4.  Persistent mild dilation of the main pulmonary artery, suggesting sequelae
of chronic pulmonary hypertension.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with severe RA, diffuse pulmonary infiltrates
with parainfluenza and concern for PCP as well as possible tracheoesophageal
fistula seen on prior CT chest, evaluate for tracheoesophageal fistula,
evaluate for change in parenchymal disease

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent and reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.25 mm
thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 34.9 cm; CTDIvol = 5.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 201.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 201 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CT dated ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE:  The visualized thyroid is extensively
calcified, likely due to underlying calcified thyroid nodules. 
Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.

MEDIASTINUM:  Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HILA:  Hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HEART:  The heart is not enlarged and there is extensive coronary arterial
calcification.  There is no pericardial effusion.  Extensive aortic valvular
mitral valvular calcification is noted as well.

VESSELS:  Vascular configuration is conventional.  Aortic caliber is normal. 
The main, right, and left pulmonary arteries are enlarged suggesting
underlying pulmonary hypertension.  A left approach PICC terminates in the low
SVC.

PULMONARY PARENCHYMA:  Extensive pulmonary parenchymal abnormality is
persistent but slightly improved from the prior study.  Extensive interstitial
thickening with underlying ground-glass opacity is worst in the right upper
lobe.  The apex and medial aspect of the right upper lobe have cleared
somewhat compared with the prior study.  Otherwise extensive chronic appearing
changes are similar.

AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. The
previously seen area concerning for tracheoesophageal fistula is less apparent
on the present study, possibly due to differences in esophageal distension or
technical artifact related to motion on the prior study (02:15, previously). 
Material along the posterior aspect of the origin of the right mainstem
bronchus may represent mucous secretions or aspirated oral content (02:23).

PLEURA:  There is are moderate bilateral right greater than left pleural
effusions.

CHEST WALL AND BONES:  There is no worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesion. 
Multilevel degenerative changes are moderate to severe.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored for evaluation of the abdomen.
Allowing for this, the partially visualized upper abdomen is notable for
partially imaged bilateral upper pole simple appearing renal cysts..

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Slight interval improvement in persistent extensive pulmonary parenchymal
abnormalities, worst in the right upper lobe.  Differential diagnosis
includes, as before, severe viral infection and acute interstitial pneumonia. 
Chronic aspiration is possible as well.
2.  Posterior defect in the trachea is not currently seen and likely
represented artifact on prior CT.  Confirmation of tracheoesophageal fistula
would require barium swallow study under fluoroscopy.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by ___
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 12:05 ___, 1 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Status post stabbing to the left flank.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical CT scan of the chest, abdomen and pelvis was
obtained after the administration of oral contrast and 130 cc IV Optiray
contrast.  Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformatted images were prepared.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  Along the right minor fissure, there is a
7-mm nodular density (2A:25), which is most compatible with a small lymph
node.  In a patient of this age, no follow up is needed.  No pleural effusions
or pneumothoraces are seen.  The airways are patent to the segmental level. 
The heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  No lymphadenopathy is seen.

CT ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The liver, spleen, bilateral kidneys,
bilateral adrenal glands, pancreas and gallbladder appear unremarkable.  The
small and large bowel are of normal size and caliber.  No abdominal free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.  

Within the left erector spinae muscle group, emphysema and areas of hematoma
are seen (301b:44).  Air tracks superiorly along the muscle groups; however,
does not appear to enter the abdominal cavity.  This can be best appreciated
on the sagittal series 301b.  A skin defect is seen in the left flank (2a:67).

CT PELVIS WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The sigmoid colon, rectum, prostate
gland, bladder, and distal ureters appear unremarkable.  No pelvic free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.

BONE WINDOWS:  No fracture or concerning lytic or sclerotic lesion is
identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No acute intra-abdominal or intrathoracic process.
2.  Left flank skin defect with underlying subcutaneous emphysema and hematoma
within the left erector spinae muscle group, consistent with history of
stabbing.

sections.indication: Status post stabbing to the left flank.
sections.technique:
Multidetector helical CT scan of the chest, abdomen and pelvis was
obtained after the administration of oral contrast and 130 cc IV Optiray
contrast.  Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformatted images were prepared.
sections.findings:
CT CHEST WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  Along the right minor fissure, there is a
7-mm nodular density (2A:25), which is most compatible with a small lymph
node.  In a patient of this age, no follow up is needed.  No pleural effusions
or pneumothoraces are seen.  The airways are patent to the segmental level. 
The heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  No lymphadenopathy is seen.

CT ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The liver, spleen, bilateral kidneys,
bilateral adrenal glands, pancreas and gallbladder appear unremarkable.  The
small and large bowel are of normal size and caliber.  No abdominal free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.  

Within the left erector spinae muscle group, emphysema and areas of hematoma
are seen (301b:44).  Air tracks superiorly along the muscle groups; however,
does not appear to enter the abdominal cavity.  This can be best appreciated
on the sagittal series 301b.  A skin defect is seen in the left flank (2a:67).

CT PELVIS WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The sigmoid colon, rectum, prostate
gland, bladder, and distal ureters appear unremarkable.  No pelvic free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.

BONE WINDOWS:  No fracture or concerning lytic or sclerotic lesion is
identified.
sections.impression:
1.  No acute intra-abdominal or intrathoracic process.
2.  Left flank skin defect with underlying subcutaneous emphysema and hematoma
within the left erector spinae muscle group, consistent with history of
stabbing.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT torso with contrast

INDICATION:  History: ___ with mechanical fall and scattere ecchymosese over
chest and abdomen.  Evaluate for fractures or hemorrhage.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:
 Total DLP (Body) = 979 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen pelvis from ___ and CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The ascending aorta appears ectatic measuring up to 3.6
cm 3.6 cm in diameter.  Mild atherosclerotic calcification is noted.  The
heart is enlarged with right chamber enlargement.  Mitral valve replacement
noted.  No pericardial effusion.
 Left upper extremity catheter tip terminates in the distal SVC.  There is
dense coronary calcification.  Patient is status post mitral valve
replacement.  The right and left atria are mildly enlarged.  The main
pulmonary and right pulmonary artery are mildly enlarged, measuring up to 3.0
cm.  Patient status post CABG.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is emphysema and mild fibrosis with scattered calcified
granulomas.  No concerning pulmonary nodule is seen.  There is evidence of
small airway disease with nodular opacities in the periphery of the lungs,
most notable in the right upper and left upper lobes.  The airways are patent
to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.  However, there is mild
bronchiectasis, especially in the bilateral lower lobes.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

Bones: Patient is status post median sternotomy with multiple intact cerclage
wires around the fractured sternum.  There is no significant bridging callus
formation in the manubrium as well as the remaining sternum.  Chronic
deformity of the left first rib at the costovertebral junction is unchanged
from prior exam.

There is bilateral gynecomastia.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates mottled enhancement, consistent with
congested liver secondary to right-sided heart disease.  Focal
hyperenhancement at the edge of segment VI (2:114) likely corresponds with
previously demonstrated hemangioma on ___. There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of laceration.  Subcentimeter hypodensity in the superior aspect of
the spleen is too small to characterize by CT (3:92).  There is a 1.5 cm
accessory spleen.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  Previously
demonstrated mass at the ileocecal valve is not seen.  There is no evidence
obstruction.  Diverticulosis of the sigmoid colon is noted, without evidence
of wall thickening and fat stranding.  The appendix is normal.  There is no
evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate is heterogeneous and enlarged.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
Extensive atherosclerotic disease with noncalcified atheroma in the abdominal
aorta is noted.  There is new ulcerated plaque in the infrarenal aorta at the
level of the origin of the ___ (3:114).

BONES: There is no acute fracture.  No focal suspicious osseous abnormality. 
There are diffuse bilateral facet arthropathy at the lumbar spine. 
Compression fracture of L1 and L2 with minimal retropulsion is unchanged from
prior exam dated ___.  Cortical deformity at the costovertebral
junction of the right eleventh rib demonstrate some callus formation, likely
reflective of chronic changes.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute sequelae of trauma.
2. Subacute and chronic fractures, detailed above.
3. Atheromatous disease of the abdominal aorta with new ulcerated plaque in
the abdominal aorta at the level of the ___.
4. Cardiomegaly with right chamber enlargement and evidence of hepatic
congestion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT ON ___ 

HISTORY:  ___ man with Hodgkin's lymphoma, ileocecal mass.  Assess
lymphoma in the chest.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
imaging of the abdomen and pelvis to be reported separately and the
intravenous infusion of 70 mL non-ionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed
as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial and 5-mm thick coronal and
parasagittal and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images, compared to prior CT scanning of
the chest on PET/CT scans since ___, most recently ___.

FINDINGS:

Central lymph nodes in the chest are numerous, and although not pathologically
enlarged, some have grown since ___, for example, in the right lower
paratracheal station, 4:74, at 10 mm, previously 4 mm, in the prevascular
station at the level of the aortopulmonic window, 4:100, now 7 mm, previously
less than 4 mm, in the subcarinal station, 2:38, now 15 mm, previously 5 mm. 
Tiny layering non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions are new and there is mild
pleural thickening in the posterior sulci bilaterally.  Previous borderline
left axillary adenopathy has resolved.  Mild peripheral interstitial pulmonary
abnormality characterized by septal thickening at the periphery of the lungs,
both in the apices and at the bases, particularly the right is difficult to
compare with previous status because earlier imaging was done at much lower
levels of resolution.  There was some abnormality at the lung bases as early
as ___, but findings suggest progression since that time.

There are no lung nodules due to malignancy.  A very small cluster of
bronchiolar nodules in the anterior segment of the left upper lobe, 4:110, is
inflammatory and not necessarily acute.  There is no consolidation or
pulmonary edema.

Findings below the diaphragm will be reported separately.  Moderately severe
L1 vertebral body compression fracture, with mild posterior protrusion of the
upper endplate, 602B:35, is unchanged since ___.

IMPRESSION:
1.  The only possible manifestation of intrathoracic lymphoma is an interval
increase in size since ___ of top normal size mediastinal lymph nodes. 
These nodes could also be a manifestation of an inflammatory reaction
associated with mild and probably progressive interstitial lung disease, which
has a pattern of usual interstitial pneumonia, but uncharacteristically slow
progression.  This could be a reflection of lung damage by chemotherapy,
depending upon clinical circumstances.  It does not look like infiltration by
lymphoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with hypoxia, +DOE, leukocytosis POD ___ s/p L
TKA// r/o PE

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT of the pulmonary arteries

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.3 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 71.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
14.3 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 4.2 s, 27.3 cm; CTDIvol = 9.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 252.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 268 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest radiograph done ___ at 12:46

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: No supraclavicular or axillary
adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN: No subdiaphragmatic pathology.

MEDIASTINUM: Subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes.

HILA: Subcentimeter hilar lymph nodes.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Normal cardiac configuration.  No pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: Trace left pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  Linear
atelectasis/scarring in the left upper lobe (series 5, image 24).  No
confluent airspace consolidation
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  The pulmonary artery is mildly dilated measuring 34 mm diameter
suggesting pulmonary hypertension.  No pulmonary arterial filling defect to
suggest pulmonary emboli.
CHEST CAGE: No lytic/destructive bony lesion.  Old lateral right rib
fractures.

IMPRESSION: 

No pulmonary emboli.  No secondary features of pulmonary emboli.

Dilated pulmonary artery suggesting pulmonary hypertension.

No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  No confluent airspace
consolidation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female presenting from hemodialysis where patient
displayed altered mental status but normal vital signs.  The patient has a
known history of diabetes, breast cancer, pulmonary sarcoid, unilateral
oophorectomy and ventral hernia.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT CT acquired axial images were obtained through the chest,
abdomen, and pelvis without intravenous or oral contrast.  Coronal and
sagittal reformations were evaluated.

Comparison is made to most recent CT examination dated ___ and
chest CT from ___

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Multiple prominent axillary
lymph nodes are identified bilaterally in addition to hilar lymphadenopathy. A
left- sided central venous catheter is noted to be in place. Atherosclerotic
disease is noted within the aorta and coronary circulation. The lungs display
small bilateral simple pleural effusions (right greater than left) with
adjacent compression atelectasis and scattered regions of linear scarring and
atelectasis. Scattered nodular opacities are noted adjacent to the hila within
the upper lobes bilaterally.  Diffuse ground glass opacity may relate to air
trapping or pulmonary edema.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Non-contrast
evaluation of the liver is unremarkable.  There is stable appearance of
cholelithiasis without evidence of acute cholecystitis.  The spleen, stomach,
small bowel, pancreas, adrenal glands, and atrophic-appearing kidneys are
unremarkable.  Extensive vascular atherosclerotic disease is again noted.  A
large ventral hernia containing non-dilated small-bowel loops is unchanged
with moderate amount of fluid again noted inferiorly within the left-sided
portion of the patient's pannus. No CT findings to suggest incarceration are
identified. Mild mesenteric stranding is redemonstrated.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Intrapelvic bowel
displays mild diverticulosis but no evidence of acute diverticulitis.  The
appendix is normal.  No abnormal free fluid is identified within the pelvic
cavity.  Urinary bladder is unremarkable.

BONES/SOFT TISSUES:  There has been extensive worsening in the degree of
anasarca within the soft tissues from prior examination.  No malignant-
appearing osseous lesions are identified.  No significant degenerative changes
are noted within the spine.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No etiology for patient's altered mental status identified.

2.  Interval development of small bilateral pleural effusions and extensive
worsening of diffuse anasarca.

3.  Extensive bilateral nodular opacities predominantly within the upper lobes
may be sequela of the patient's known sarcoidosis or represent an acute
infectious process.  Scattered ground-glass opacities, resulting in mosaic
attenuation in the lung is likely related to underlying airtrapping or mild
alveolar edema.  Would consider repeat chest CT when patient is stable to
better stage sarcoid if alteration in management will occur.

4.  Unchanged large ventral hernia without CT findings to suggest
incarceration/strangulation although this is a clinical diagnosis.

5. Cholelithiasis.

chest CT from ___

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Multiple prominent axillary
lymph nodes are identified bilaterally in addition to hilar lymphadenopathy. A
left- sided central venous catheter is noted to be in place. Atherosclerotic
disease is noted within the aorta and coronary circulation. The lungs display
small bilateral simple pleural effusions (right greater than left) with
adjacent compression atelectasis and scattered regions of linear scarring and
atelectasis. Scattered nodular opacities are noted adjacent to the hila within
the upper lobes bilaterally.  Diffuse ground glass opacity may relate to air
trapping or pulmonary edema.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Non-contrast
evaluation of the liver is unremarkable.  There is stable appearance of
cholelithiasis without evidence of acute cholecystitis.  The spleen, stomach,
small bowel, pancreas, adrenal glands, and atrophic-appearing kidneys are
unremarkable.  Extensive vascular atherosclerotic disease is again noted.  A
large ventral hernia containing non-dilated small-bowel loops is unchanged
with moderate amount of fluid again noted inferiorly within the left-sided
portion of the patient's pannus. No CT findings to suggest incarceration are
identified. Mild mesenteric stranding is redemonstrated.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS OR ORAL CONTRAST:  Intrapelvic bowel
displays mild diverticulosis but no evidence of acute diverticulitis.  The
appendix is normal.  No abnormal free fluid is identified within the pelvic
cavity.  Urinary bladder is unremarkable.

BONES/SOFT TISSUES:  There has been extensive worsening in the degree of
anasarca within the soft tissues from prior examination.  No malignant-
appearing osseous lesions are identified.  No significant degenerative changes
are noted within the spine.
sections.impression:
1.  No etiology for patient's altered mental status identified.

2.  Interval development of small bilateral pleural effusions and extensive
worsening of diffuse anasarca.

3.  Extensive bilateral nodular opacities predominantly within the upper lobes
may be sequela of the patient's known sarcoidosis or represent an acute
infectious process.  Scattered ground-glass opacities, resulting in mosaic
attenuation in the lung is likely related to underlying airtrapping or mild
alveolar edema.  Would consider repeat chest CT when patient is stable to
better stage sarcoid if alteration in management will occur.

4.  Unchanged large ventral hernia without CT findings to suggest
incarceration/strangulation although this is a clinical diagnosis.

5. Cholelithiasis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with infiltrate  // r/o mass

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast chest CT was performed acquiring sequential axial
images from the thoracic inlet through the adrenal glands. Thin section axial,
coronal, sagittal and axial MIP's were also obtained.

DOSE:  Total DLP = 454.76mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid gland is unremarkable. Multiple small mediastinal lymph nodes
measuring up to 9 mm in short axis in the right lower paratracheal location
are slightly larger as compared to ___. There are no pathologically
enlarged supraclavicular, mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymph nodes.

Heart size is top-normal with no pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary
artery is normal caliber. The right pulmonary artery is dilated measuring 2.9
cm. The left pulmonary artery is top-normal caliber measuring 2.7 cm.  This is
of uncertain significance. Extensive coronary artery and aortic valvular
calcifications are present. Heavy mitral annular calcifications are also
noted.

Moderate bronchial wall thickening is most extensive in the lower lobes. There
is also circumferential wall thickening diffusely involving the trachea and
mainstem bronchi with presence of a focal calcification in wall a proximal
with main bronchus (4, 104). Secretions layer dependently in the lower trachea
and bilateral main bronchus. Right lower lobe predominant peribronchial
ground-glass opacities are likely infectious or inflammatory in etiology.
Solid pulmonary micronodules measuring no more than 3 mm are stable since ___, and may be presumed benign (4: 35, 37, 46, 95). Upper lobe predominant
centrilobular nodules are again noted. Right middle lobe subpleural
reticulation may be due to a non-characteristic interstitial abnormality.

The lower esophageal wall shows asymmetric nodular wall thickening (4, 186).
Images of the upper abdomen show nonspecific gaseous distension of the stomach
and colon. A small right hepatic lobe cyst is incidentally noted.

Spinal degenerative changes are most extensive in the cervical region. There
are no bone lesions in the thorax worrisome for infection or malignancy. Mild
kyphosis and mild dextroscoliosis of the thoracolumbar spine is unchanged.

IMPRESSION: 

Increased diffuse tracheal and bilateral mainstem bronchial wall thickening
with new focal calcification raises concern for airway involvement by
Wegener's granulomatosis or amyloidosis.

Right lower lobe predominant peribronchial ground-glass opacities may be due
to infection or aspiration.

Upper lobe predominant centrilobular nodules may be due to infection or
respiratory bronchiolitis.

Nodular asymmetric wall thickening of the lower esophagus warrants endoscopic
correlation is advised to exclude a mass.

Extensive coronary artery and aortic valvular calcifications.

NOTIFICATION:  The impression and recommendation above was entered by Dr.
___ on ___ at 16:42 into the Department of Radiology critical
communications system for direct communication to the referring provider.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with obstructive PFTs and scattered GGO and
airway thickening on ___ CT.  // Eval for interval change, infiltrates

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  447 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum.  There
are multiple normal sized mediastinal lymph nodes (2, 16). Moderate aortic
wall calcifications, mild dilatation of the main pulmonary artery, potentially
suggestive of pulmonary hypertension.  Severe coronary calcifications, mild
aortic valve calcifications, severe mitral valve calcifications, no
pericardial effusion.  Mild hiatal hernia.  8 mm liver cysts (2, 58).  No
acute abnormalities in the upper abdomen.  No osteolytic lesions at the level
of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral bodies.  Mild degenerative
vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression fractures.  Old healed rib
fracture (602 B, 26).  Non characteristic scarring at the lung apices.  New
right upper lobe subpleural granuloma (4, 47).  Several millimetric pulmonary
nodules, mostly subpleural in location, an with benign morphology (4, 59). 
Signs of mild to moderate chronic airways disease with overinflation. 
Ground-glass density 3 mm perifissural right-sided nodule (4, 97).  Non
characteristic scarring at the bases of the middle lobe.  Stable scarring at
the bases of the middle lobe and the lingular as well as the left lower lobe. 
Areas of non characteristic ground-glass opacities continue to be seen at the
bases of the right lower lobe (4, 187).

IMPRESSION: 

No relevant change as compared to ___.  Signs of severe chronic
airways disease, with lower lobe predominant but overall generalized
overinflation as well as infectious or inflammatory peribronchial parenchymal
reactions, predominating in the right lower lobe (4, 190).

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with COPD, scattered < 4 mm nodules on ___
scan// ___ year f/u for pulm nodules

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.1 s, 39.9 cm; CTDIvol = 5.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 229.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 230 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Unremarkable.  Partially imaged
thyroid gland is homogeneous.

UPPER ABDOMEN: 11 mm hypodensity in the liver on series 2, image 60 is stable
compared to today's measurement of the prior study.  There is a small hiatal
hernia.

MEDIASTINUM: Scattered small mediastinal lymph nodes are not pathologically
enlarged.

HILA: No hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Coronary artery and aortic valve calcifications are
seen.  There are also coarse mitral annulus calcifications.  No pericardial
effusion is seen.  Aortic calcifications are seen.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen.  Minimal biapical
pleuroparenchymal scarring is seen.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Several millimetric, sub 4 mm pulmonary nodules are
re-demonstrated in the right upper lobe, stable since the prior study,
including on series 4, image 55 4, image 81.  Re-demonstrated is stable
chronic changes/scarring at the inferior right middle lobe, lingula, and
anterior left lung base.  Subtle perifissural ground-glass opacity on series
4, image 133 is stable since the prior study.  Subtle areas of ground-glass
opacity at the inferior right lower lobe are less conspicuous as compared to
the prior study.
2. Subtle 6 mm ground-glass opacity in the superior left lower lobe on series
4, image 111, is stable.  There is bilateral bronchial wall thickening,
similar to prior.  No new pulmonary nodule or new focal consolidation is seen.
3. AIRWAYS: Central airways are patent.
4. VESSELS:  Aortic calcifications are seen.  No thoracic aortic aneurysm.
CHEST CAGE: Multilevel degenerative changes along the spine are grossly
stable.  No acute fracture seen.

IMPRESSION: 

Overall stable examination compared to the prior study.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Shortness of breath, hypotension and fever.  Question pulmonary
emboli.

COMPARISONS:  Chest radiograph from earlier on the same day was unremarkable
aside from minor bibasilar atelectasis.  No prior dedicated chest CT imaging
is available.

TECHNIQUE:  Following a low-dose non-contrast scan of the chest, a CT
angiogram was performed with intravenous contrast.  Sagittal, coronal, and
bilateral oblique projection reconstructions were also performed.

Shortly after the study, a preliminary interpretation was provided by Dr.
___ at 8:30 p.m. which stated "non-diagnostic study secondary to poor bolus
timing.  Questionable areas of hypodensity in segment lower lobe PA branches
appear artifactual.  However, PE cannot be excluded and the study should be
repeated or VQ scan obtained.  Bibasilar atelectasis.  Nodular liver with
heterogeneous enhancement, and splenomegaly, likely related to cirrhosis and
portal hypertension.  Small free fluid in the abdomen.  Discussed with Dr.
___

CT OF THE CHEST WITH AND WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  A right internal
jugular venous catheter terminates in the upper portion of the superior vena
cava, as before.  

There are no enlarged lymph nodes.  No pericardial effusion is present.  

Coronary artery calcifications are present.  There are also annular
calcifications about the aortic valve and even denser ones involving the
annulus of the mitral valve.  The heart appears mildly enlarged.  There is
mild bilateral gynecomastia.  

Although the pulmonary arteries are not optimally opacified for evaluation, no
discrete suspicious filling defects are identified.  Aside from minor
bibasilar atelectasis or scarring, the lungs are otherwise clear.  Airways
serving the lower lobes appear minimally thickened.  A calcified plaque or
perhaps a post-operative site in the left lower lobe is noted with linear
densities and minor scarring or atelectasis.  

The liver appears nodular, suggesting cirrhosis, including enlargement of the
left lateral segments and caudate.  The spleen is enlarged.  A widely patent
umbilical vein is partly visualized.  A small amount of ascites is visualized
about the liver.

A partly exophytic nodule along the liver dome measures 24 x 23 mm in axial
___ (3:86)and shows suspicious arterial enhancment, for which there is
substantial suspicion for hepatocellular carcinoma.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or blastic bone lesions.  Small
anterior osteophytes are present along the anterior margin of the mid-to-lower
thoracic spine.  Partly visualized mild-to-moderate cervical spondylosis is
also noted along the lower cervical spine.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism on limited examination, noting poor
bolus timing.  If there is continuing clinical concern for pulmonary emboli, a
V/Q scan could be considered as an alternative.

2.  Nodular liver with sequelae of portal hypertension.

3.  Enhancing exophytic mass in the hepatic dome, concerning for
hepatocellular carcinoma.  When clinically appropriate, further evaluation
with multiphasic CT or MR is recommended, as well as clinical correlation
including AFP level.

Final report discussed with Dr. ___ on the same day by telephone.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:      ___ MAN WITH CARCINOMA OF UNKNOWN PRIMARY.

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS COORDINATED WITH
INTRAVENOUS INFUSION OF NONIONIC CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5
AND 1.25 MM THICK AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP
AXIAL IMAGES. SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS AND THE TOTAL
DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  PET CT ___.

FINDINGS: 

25 mm wide homogeneous soft tissue nodule with perimeter enhancement in the
left supraclavicular station, was 12 x 14 mm in ___, probably a malignant
lymph node. Other supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, hilar, and

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with follow up lung cancer. follow up lung
cancer.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agentand reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 577 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Comparison is made to multiple prior studies, most recently from
___, dating back to chest CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

MEDIASTINUM: The thyroid is normal. Intermediate density lobulated prevascular
mediastinal mass measures 3.2 x 2.2 cm, not significantly changed in axial
dimension when measured at a similar level compared to the prior study
(4:130), but has slowly grown since most remote available imaging from ___.
There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The aorta is normal in caliber throughout its intrathoracic course.
Enlargement of the intra mediastinal right pulmonary artery up to 33 mm is
unchanged compared to the prior study, and suggestive of underlying pulmonary
arterial hypertension (02:29). Mild Coronary arterial and aortic annular
calcifications are present.  The heart size is normal and there is no
pericardial effusion.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax.  Scarring along the lateral right lung base
is unchanged.

LUNGS: Postsurgical changes related to prior right upper lobectomy are again
noted, with unremarkable appearance of the right upper lobe bronchial stump
(04:105). A subtle small area of ground-glass in the right upper lobe is
unchanged since at least ___ (4:78). Multiple bilateral pulmonary
nodules are small, many of which are calcified, and stable over multiple prior
exams (04:25, 36, 165, 190, 180, 199, 215, 234). No new or enlarging pulmonary
nodules are identified. There is no airspace consolidation.  There is no
diffuse interstitial abnormality.

BONES: Multilevel degenerative changes are noted in the thoracic spine.  Right
thoracotomy changes are again noted.  No lytic or blastic lesion suspicious
for malignancy is identified in the chest cage.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  Although this study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic
evaluation, limited upper abdominal assessment is unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:



1. No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.
2. Intermediate density prevascular mediastinal mass is stable since the prior
study, and minimally increased in size since ___, most likely benign.
3. Multiple small bilateral pulmonary nodules are stable. No new or growing
nodules are identified.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with follow up nodules and history of lung cancer
// follow up nodules and history of lung cancer

TECHNIQUE:   Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.
DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP 294.3mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  Chest CT scanning since ___, most recently ___ and ___.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged and there are no
soft tissue abnormalities in the chest wall suspicious for malignancy.  This
study is not appropriate for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis but shows no adrenal
mass.

Atherosclerotic calcification is not apparent in head neck vessels and is mild
in the coronaries at least in the LAD.  Aorta and pulmonary arteries are
normal size.  Size of cardiac chambers, particularly ventricles is not
possible to evaluate.  There is no pericardial or pleural abnormality.

Thyroid is unremarkable.  Crescentic thickening of the posterior wall of the
upper trachea at the level of the thoracic inlet, involving both the
cartilaginous and membranous region, reaching maximum thickness of 7 mm, over
length of roughly 9mm, 05:47-54, is new or substantially increased since ___, 04:38.  It could be inspissated mucus, but the attenuation
characteristics are unreliable.  An adjacent 6 mm wide lymph node in the upper
tracheoesophageal groove, 05:58, is unchanged.  A prevascular 7 mm node,
5:121, is probably new.  Sub carinal sub cm nodes are stable.

The homogeneous 25 x 39 mm soft tissue mass in the prevascular mediastinum
adjacent to the main pulmonary artery has been growing extremely slowly since
at least ___ when it was 24 x 18mm.  The largest diameters on ___
were 24 x 36 mm, 4:127.

Right hilus has a normal postoperative appearance following upper lobectomy,
including others a bulbous contour adjacent to the right middle lobe bronchus.
There is no good evidence for hilar adenopathy or local recurrence.

Lung lesions are as follows:

4 mm right lower lobe nodule, 5:100, unchanged since ___, was a mixed
density lesion with a smaller soft tissue component in ___, 4:63.  It
should be followed closely for possible early malignancy.  Pleural thickening
and adjacent subpleural fibrosis in the right lower lobe are probably post
surgical, unchanged since ___.  There no focal lesions in the left lung of
concern.

There no bone lesions in the chest cage suspicious for malignancy or
infection.

.


IMPRESSION: 

New wall thickening, upper trachea could be inspissated mucus or mucosal
lesion, should be evaluated with bronchoscopy.

4 mm right upper lobe lung nodule although stable since ___ was
previously a mixed density lesion in should be monitored carefully to
distinguish an early carcinoma from a postinflammatory lesion.

No evidence of recurrent or  metastatic malignancy related to previous right
upper lobectomy.

Continued slow growth to prevascular mediastinal soft tissue mass has been
present since at least ___ suggests this is an encapsulated thymoma.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Bronchoscopy.

Surgical evaluation regarding possible mediastinal thymoma.

supraclavicular or and no axillary lymphadenopathy.  Chest wall with no soft
tissue abnormalities.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Tiny calculus in the upper pole of the right kidney with no
evidence of hydronephrosis.  Remaining included upper abdominal organs are
unremarkable.

MEDIASTINUM: No mediastinal and no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart is normal in size.  Mild hypodensity of the
cardiac chambers relative to the septum suggests minimal anemia.  Specks of

normal in diameter.

PLEURA: There are no pleural space abnormalities.

LUNG: Previously described thickening of the posterior wall of upper trachea
at the level of the thoracic inlet resolved, representing mucous retention.

lobectomy,

0.4 cm right upper lobe nodule with minimal ground-glass opacity has been

Right upper lobe 0.4 cm nodule with minimal surrounding ground-glass opacity

hypertension.  Yet there are no corresponding lung findings to explain.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Evaluate lung lesion seen in spine CT

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent  reconstructed as
axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Reported in the concurrent abdomen CT in

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid has sub cm hypodense nodule in the right lobe and hyperdense
subcentimeter nodule in the left lobe. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal
and hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are
normal size. Cardiac configuration is normal and there is no appreciable
coronary calcification.
Irregular spiculated nodule in the left apex has a solid component that
measures 9 x 13 mm, its peripheral ground-glass component is difficult to
measure, aprox 32x16 mm (7:47)
Bibasilar dependent atelectasis are larger on the right side.  There is right
apical scarring.
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.
Please refer to the concurrent abdomen CT for complete description of the
intra-abdominal findings.
There are no bone findings of malignancy

IMPRESSION: 

Spiculated left apical semi-solid lesion concerning for lung malignancy.

axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 437

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid has hypodense nodules as before. . Supraclavicular, axillary,
mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary
arteries are normal size. Cardiac configuration is normal and there is no
appreciable coronary calcification. There is no pleural or pericardial
effusion.
Semi-solid spiculated nodule in the left apex still worrisome for malignancy

Right IJ catheter tip is in the cavoatrial junction
This examination is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation the upper
abdomen is normal

There are no new lung nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX

INDICATION:  Right upper lobe spiculated mixed low-attenuation nodules. 
Dyspnea on exertion.  Evaluation of pulmonary status.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in prone and
supine position.  The expiratory acquisitions, coronal reconstructions, no
administration of intravenous contrast material.

FINDINGS:  The examination is compared to ___.

The right upper lobe, partly solid and partly non-solid nodule (104, 48) is
unchanged in size and morphology.  The nodule still shows few spiculations and
has a moderately solid center.  The morphologic characteristics of the nodule
still could suggest malignancy.

The pre-existing 8 mm ground-glass nodule in the left upper lobe has markedly
decreased in size and now seen with a 2-mm diameter (104, 52).

All other lung nodules, mostly consisting of pleural millimetric granulomas,
are unchanged.

Also unchanged in size and extent is a group of relatively homogeneous
pulmonary nodules in the left upper lobe in paramediastinal location (104, 75.

There is no evidence of newly appeared suspicious lung nodules.

Unchanged areas of basal scarring.

Unchanged absence of pleural effusion.  Unchanged coronary calcifications, no
hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  No pleural effusions.  Unchanged
generalized irregularities and thickening of the bronchial walls, without
evidence of endobronchial obstructions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  All preexisting nodules are of unchanged size and morphology except for a
left upper lobe ground-glass nodule that has decreased in size.  Because the
spiculated right upper lobe nodule nevertheless still displays findings
suggestive of malignancy, CT followup should be continued in approximately six
months to rule out growth of this nodule.
2.  No newly appeared lung nodules.
3.  Unchanged basal parenchymal scarring.
4.  No lymphadenopathy, no pleural effusions.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with pulmonary masses.  For followup.

COMPARISON:  Multiple chest CTs of ___, and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT acquired images were obtained from thoracic inlet through the
upper abdomen after the administration of 100 cc of Optiray intravenous
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also displayed.

FINDINGS:  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none meeting CT criteria for
pathologic enlargement, are unchanged since the prior study.  There is no
hilar or axillary lymphadenopathy.  The heart size is normal without
pericardial effusion. Surgical clips within the right breast are consistent
with prior lumpectomy.  

The partly solid and ground-glass spiculated nodule in the right upper lobe
(4:37) is unchanged in size and appearance since the prior study.

Multiple other small pulmonary nodules are unchanged.  These include a 4-mm
nodule (4:129) and multiple other 1-2 mm nodules (4:164, 192, 206) in the
right lower lobe and a calcified granuloma in the right upper lobe (4:140). No
new pulmonary nodules are identified.

Multiple ___ opacities are present throughout the lungs in addition to
tracheal and bronchial secretions, consistent with underlying airways disease.

This examination is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation.  There is a
smooth nonaggressive-appearing 1.6 cm mass within the lower esophageal wall
just above the GE junction with a central dystrophic calcification, unchanged
in appearance since ___, and may represent a benign leiomyoma. 
Dystrophic calcifications are also noted at the posterior wall of the gastric
fundus, unchanged.

BONE WINDOWS:  No concerning osseous lesions are identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No interval change over the past year of a spiculated right upper lobe
nodule; however, this nodule continues to display characterstics worrisome for
malignancy and may represent a very slow growing malignancy.  Followup exam is
recommended in ___ months.

2.  Multiple other small pulmonary nodules are unchanged in size and
appearance.  No new pulmonary nodules identified.

3.  Stable appearance of nonaggressive-appearing 1.6 cm mass in the lower
esophageal wall with central area of dystrophic calcification which may
represent a benign leiomyoma.

4.  Diffuse ___ opacities with secretions in the trachea and bronchi
consistent with underlying airways disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Followup of spiculated lesion in the right lung.

COMPARISON:  CTs ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric multidetector CT acquisition of the chest was performed
without intravenous contrast.  Images are presented for display in the axial
plane at 5-mm and 1.25-mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar reformation
images are submitted for review.

FINDINGS:  The 2.2 x 1.3 cm, mixed solid and ground-glass, spiculated lesion
in the right upper lobe was 2.0 x 0.9 cm ___ years ago.  A 7-mm satellite
lesion is new (4:42). Multiple other small pulmonary nodules are unchanged. 
For example, a 4-mm right lower lobe nodule (4:121), 1-2 mm nodules in the
right lower lobe (4:157, 194), and a calcified granuloma (4:146) are
unchanged.  A 9-mm left lower lobe ground-glass opacity (4:137) was present on
prior studies, but is slightly more conspicuous than on ___ and
___.  Multifocal ___ opacities throughout both lungs are again
noted. One region in the left upper lobe now has a 5-mm nodule (4:84) which is
new or larger than prior. The remainder of the opacities are similar. 
Findings suggest non-tuberculosis mycobacterial infection. 

Scarring in the left lower lobe is similar to the prior study. There is
biapical pleuroparenchymal scarring. Mediastinal lymph nodes are borderline
enlarged ranging up to 11 mm in the precarinal station.  

The thoracic aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.
Atherosclerotic calcifications are seen at the aortic arch and at the origins
of the head and neck vessels. The left atrium is enlarged.  Coronary artery
calcifications are of unknown hemodynamic significance.  There is no pleural
or pericardial effusion. Surgical clips in the right breast are again noted.
No nodules are seen in the thyroid gland. 

This study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation. Smooth eccentric
wall thickening of the distal esophagus with a calcification is unchanged and
may be due to a benign leiomyoma.  Dystrophic calcifications in the posterior
wall of the gastric fundus are also unchanged.  Left adrenal thickening is
stable.  No right adrenal lesion is seen.  

No bone finding suspicious for infection or malignancy is seen.

IMPRESSION:
1. Multifocal ___ opacities are largely similar to the prior study
with a new 5-mm nodule in the left upper lobe. The findings suggest
non-tuberculosis mycobacterial infection (___).

2. The spiculated right apical lesion is larger with a new 7mm satellite
lesion.  Before assuming this lesion is a slowly growing malignancy, consider
that this may be an atypical representation of non-tubercular mycobacterium
given diffuse ___ opacities.

3.  Stable appearance of a non-aggressive-appearing eccentric mass in the
lower esophagus with central dystrophic calcification which may be a benign
leiomyoma.

4.  Multiple unchanged sub-5mm pulmonary nodules.

The thoracic aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.
Atherosclerotic calcifications are seen at the aortic arch and at the origins

may be due to a benign leiomyoma.  Dystrophic calcifications in the posterior
wall of the gastric fundus are also unchanged.  Left adrenal thickening is
stable.  No right adrenal lesion is seen.  

No bone finding suspicious for infection or malignancy is seen.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WANDW/O C

INDICATION:  ___ man with febrile neutropenia and lung nodiules found
incidentally on neck CT presenting for further evaluation of pulmonary
nodules.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 420 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CT is available on PACS at the time of this
dictation.  Reference is made to a CT neck dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

Detailed evaluation of the solid organs, soft tissues, and vessels is limited
without the use of intravenous contrast. Within this limitation:

The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of atherosclerotic
calcified disease.  The main, left, and right pulmonary arteries are normal in
caliber.  The heart appears normal in size.  Hypoattenuation of the cardiac
blood pool on this unenhanced exam indicates anemia.  No evidence of a
pericardial effusion.  The right Port-A-Cath tip ends in the proximal right
atrium.

No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.

Numerous, small bilateral pulmonary nodules and are nonspecific favor
infectious in etiology since some of the nodules in the previously imaged
upper lungs appear new in the short interval and given the patient's history
of neutropenia and fever.  A right lower lobe pulmonary nodule measures 10 mm
(series 4, image 25).  A lingular subpleural nodule measures 7 mm (series 4,
image 222).  A calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe measures 5 mm
(series 4, image 283).  The airways are patent to at least the subsegmental
level.  No evidence of bronchiectasis or significant peribronchiolar wall
thickening.  No pneumothorax or pleural effusion. No cavitary lesions.

The thyroid gland is normal in size without evidence of focal mass.

No osseous lesions in the chest cage concerning for malignancy.  Degenerative
changes in the upper thoracic spine are mild.  No evidence of acute fracture.

This exam is not dedicated for imaging of the upper abdomen.  Within this
limitation:  The spleen appears normal in size.  Diverticulosis of the
partially imaged transverse colon in the upper abdomen is mild.  Partially
imaged small bowel loops in the left upper abdomen containing small amount of
fluid but are normal in caliber.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Numerous bilateral pulmonary nodules appear increased in number at least
in the upper lobes since the prior neck CT and favor infectious etiology,
likely fungal in the setting of febrile neutropenia.  Correlate with clinical
assessment.  If the patient's symptoms persist despite treatment, consider
repeat Chest CT in ___ weeks to reevaluate.

2.  Anemia.

3.  Minimal colonic diverticulosis.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  If the patient's symptoms persist despite treatment,
consider repeat Chest CT in ___ weeks to reevaluate.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings, impression, and recommendation were discussed
with ___, M.D. by ___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at
4:39 ___, 20 minutes after discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT chest without contrast

INDICATION:  Mr. ___ us a ___ year old male with history of AML on HiDAC C4D1
(___) who initially presented with febrile neutropenia and associated
worsening cellulitis under the L mandible found to have MSSA bacteremia now
s/p port removal on cefazolin// please eval for pulm nodules

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  The heart,
pericardium, and great vessels are within normal limits based on an unenhanced
scan. No pericardial effusion is seen.  There has been interval removal of
Port-A-Cath since comparisons study.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:

There are multiple scattered pulmonary nodules in both lungs, most of which
are mildly decreased in size as compared to chest CT ___.  For
example, a left upper lobe nodule now measures 3 mm (5:67), previously 4 mm. 
A sub solid nodule in the Left upper lobe measures 6 mm (5:117), previously 7
mm.  A Left major perifissural nodule measures 6 mm (5:200) previously 7 mm. 
A spiculated nodule in the superior segment of the right lower lobe measures 5
mm (5:169), previously 10 mm.  Another right lower lobe nodule measures 3 mm
(5:190), previously 6 mm.  A right upper lobe nodule measures 3 mm (5:73),
previously 6 mm.  There is no evidence of new pulmonary nodules or nodules
that are increasing in size.  No focal consolidation.

The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally. 
There is no bronchiectasis.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: There is mild diverticulosis of the partially visualized colon
without evidence of diverticulitis.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.


IMPRESSION: 

Multiple scattered pulmonary nodules are minimally decreased in size as
compared to chest CT ___.  No new pulmonary nodules identified.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with new diagnosis of pancreatic cancer//
Initial staging exam, r/o pulm mets

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.6 s, 36.5 cm; CTDIvol = 14.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 512.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 512 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST PERIMETER: Thoracic outlet is obscured by metal prosthesis artifact from
shoulder replacements.  Thyroid is moderately enlarged but there are no focal
findings warranting further imaging.  Axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged. 
Breast evaluation is reserved exclusively for mammography.  No soft tissue
abnormalities elsewhere in the chest wall.  This study is not designed for
subdiaphragmatic diagnosis.  Posterior diaphragmatic eventration or hernia is
transmit only subphrenic fat, clinically insignificant.

CARDIO-MEDIASTINUM:Esophagus is unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic calcification
is not apparent head and neck vessels, minimal in left anterior descending
coronary artery.  Heart is not enlarged.  Pericardium is physiologic.  Main
and right pulmonary arteries are mildly enlarged, 34 mm and 27 mm
respectively.

THORACIC LYMPH NODES: Is no lymph nodes in the chest are pathologically
enlarged.

LUNGS, AIRWAYS, PLEURAE: Emphysema is moderate to severe, right upper lobe,
milder elsewhere.

Solitary 7 mm subpleural solid nodule, right lower lobe, 4:163.  Adjacent
pleura is not thickened.

CHEST CAGE: Impression of the upper endplate of the first lumbar vertebral
body is responsible for moderate loss of height.  Chest cage is otherwise
unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

Solitary 7 mm right lower lobe lung nodule in a patient with moderate to
severe emphysema.  Suggest repeat chest CT in 6 months, according to following
criteria:

follow-up in 6 to 12 months, and a CT in ___ months is recommended.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

Possible pulmonary arterial hypertension, attributable to emphysema.

radiology_facts.report_text:
REASON FOR EXAMINATION:  Evaluation of the patient with acute leukemia and
breast mass.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained from thoracic inlet to upper
abdomen with subsequent 1.25- and 5-mm collimation axial images reviewed in
conjunction with coronal and sagittal reformats.

FINDINGS:

Assessment of the chest reveals left breast nodule, solid and
well-circumscribed, approximately 15.1 x 12.8 mm in diameter, 2:34,
corresponding to the nodule demonstrated on the mammography.  No axillary
pathologically enlarged lymph nodes demonstrated, although several small lymph
nodes are present.  No other nodules within the breast demonstrated.  Neither
ipsi- nor contralaterally.  No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is seen. 
Aberrant right subclavian artery is demonstrated, anatomical variant.  Cluster
of lymph nodes is noted at the lower aspect of the right axilla, again not
pathologically enlarged but multiple.

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are unremarkable.  No pericardial or pleural
effusion is seen.  The imaged portion of the upper abdomen reveals no
appreciable abnormality.

For the assessment of the thoracic inlet review CT neck and the corresponding
report.

There are no bone lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. Assessment of the
lung parenchyma demonstrates several pulmonary nodules:  4:70, 4:136,
4:60,lingular nodule, 4:212.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Left breast nodule as described that should be further correlated with
tissue biopsy.
2.  Right axillary lymph nodes, not pathologically enlarged but multiple.
3.  Several pulmonary nodules that should be reassessed in three months for
assessment of stability.
