radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ man with AML and ongoing neutropenic fever associated
with cough despite broad-spectrum antibiotics.

COMPARISON:  Comparison is made to CT of the chest from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT images are obtained of the thorax without the use of
intravenous contrast.  Reformatted coronal, sagittal and axial maximum
intensity projection images were reviewed.

CT THORAX WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  Numerous bilateral axillary lymph nodes are
not pathologically enlarged, and are stable to decreased in size since the
prior study (3:14).  No supraclavicular lymphadenopathy is present.  Multiple
prominent mediastinal lymph nodes are not  pathologically enlarged, the
measuring up to 6 mm in short axis diameter in the precarinal station (3:13). 
No pleural or pericardial effusion is identified.  The heart and pericardium
are unremarkable.  The intrathoracic aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal
in caliber.

A left breast nodule is again seen (5:154) and intervally decreased in size
since the prior study, now measuring 13 x 11 mm, previously 15 x 13 mm,
consistent with a hematoma, as suggested on diagnostic mammogram and
ultrasound from ___.

Lung windows demonstrate interval development of numerous bilateral semisolid
(5:91) and ground-glass nodules  (5:57), which are randomly distributed in all
lobes of the lungs and range from 1 mm to 3 mm in size (5:134).  Subsegmental
bibasilar atelectasis is also present.  A punctate calcified granuloma is
identified in the right lower lobe (5:171).   A trace left pleural effusion is
present (5:206).

Although the study is not designed for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
structures, the unenhanced appearance of the upper abdomen is unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No lytic or blastic lesion suspicious for malignancy is
identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Numerous small ground glass and semi-solid pulmonary nodules are randomly
distributed throughout all lobes of both lungs and are new since the prior
study.  Although this finding is not concerning for malignant disease, their
presence in a neutropenic patient could represent an inflammatory or
infectious process such as viral pneumonia.  No evidence of bacterial or
fungal pneumonia.
2.  Interval decrease in size of left breast nodule, in keeping with a
hematoma as described on recent diagnostic mammogram from ___.
3.  Numerous bilateral axillary and mediastinal lymph nodes are not
pathologically enlarged.
4.  Trace left pleural effusion (5:206).

The above findings were communicated to Dr. ___ by Dr. ___ telephone
at 4:15 p.m., within five minutes of discovery.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History of AML, neutropenic fevers, awaiting allotransplant. 
Pleuritic chest pain.

COMPARISONS:  Chest CT from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial imaging was obtained through the chest following the
administration of intravenous contrast material.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats were completed.

FINDINGS:  The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  There are no enlarged
supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes.  There is a small
new pericardial effusion, simple in nature.  A right chest wall port catheter
tip terminates in the right atrium.  There is no fluid collection around the
catheter course.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.

There is a large area of consolidation within the right upper lobe abutting
the fissure, 4:136, with surrounding ground glass opacities.  Multiple
additional pulmonary opacities, for example in the anterior right upper lobe,
4:102; superior segment of the left upper lobe, 4:134, as well as a focal area
of consolidation in the right lower lobe measuring 2.1 x 2.1 cm (4:95) are
most consistent with multifocal infection.  A 9 x 9 mm nodule in the right
apex, 4:60, is also likely part of the same process and has ground glass
opacities surrounding it.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 
Bibasilar atelectasis is noted.

This study is not tailored for evaluation of subdiaphragmatic structures. 
Limited views are unremarkable.

There are no suspicious bony lesions.

IMPRESSION:  
1.  Multifocal pulmonary opacities, the largest of which is in the right upper
lobe, with surrounding ground glass opacities concerning for fungal infection
such as invasive aspergillosis.  

2.  Small pericardial effusion, new since the prior exam.

These findings were discussed with Dr. ___ by Dr. ___
telephone at 1:30 p.m.

posterior segment of the right upper lobe (5:146) has changed confirmation,

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with AML s/p allo stem cell transplant, with new
SOB x 1 week. CXR today abnormal.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 370mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph dated the same day. Prior chest CT dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

Soft tissues:The thyroid is homogeneous. Minimally prominent bilateral
axillary, upper paratracheal, prevascular, and right lower paratracheal lymph
nodes are minimally larger than the comparison CT from ___. The heart is
normal in size and there is no pericardial effusion. The aorta and main
pulmonary artery are normal in caliber.  Of note, there is a normal caliber,
aberrant retroesophageal right subclavian artery.   Although the study is not
tailored for evaluation of the subdiaphragmatic regions, limited evaluation of
the abdomen is unremarkable.

Lungs:The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. There are
diffuse bilateral subpleural and peribronchovascular opacities, some in a
nodular configuration, predominantly in the lower lobes and at the lung
peripherally, although also seen in the upper lobes. Additionally, there are
more confluent areas of opacity, particularly at the left lung base and at the
anterior aspect of the right upper lobe (5:119).  There is no pneumothorax or
pleural effusion.

Bones:No blastic or lytic lesions suspicious for malignancy or infection.

IMPRESSION: 

Diffuse bilateral pulmonary opacities predominantly in the lower lobes and at
the periphery of the lungs, which may be seen with multifocal infection, such
as a viral infection. In the setting of allogeneic bone marrow transplant and
minimal patient symptoms, cryptogenic organizing pneumonia, a diagnosis of
exclusion, may be most likely in this situation.

NOTIFICATION:  These findings were relayed to ___ by Dr. ___
telephone at 14:07 on ___, 10 min after discovery.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with left breast cancer and AML, s/p allo
transplant ___.  With abnormal CT chest on ___, on voriconazole since;
f/u of abnormal CT ___

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast chest CT was performed acquiring sequential axial
images from the thoracic inlet through the adrenal glands. Thin section axial,
coronal, sagittal and axial MIP's were also obtained.

DOSE:  Total DLP = 405.72mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___ and ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid gland is unremarkable. Multiple mildly prominent mediastinal lymph
nodes measuring up to 5 mm in short axis are stable.  There is no
supraclavicular, hilar or axillary lymphadenopathy.

Heart size is normal with no pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary artery
and thoracic aorta are normal caliber.

The majority of the previously seen bilateral upper lobe peripheral opacities
have improved or completely resolved since ___. Some of the more
discrete nodular opacities in the bilateral lower lobes have also resolved.
However, basal and peripheral predominant opacities now have a more
peribronchovascular and perilobular distribution (4, 203 - 205). There is also
new mild traction bronchiectasis ,which is most pronounced in the right middle
lobe and lingula.  Anterior right upper lobe subsegmental atelectasis, volume
loss, bronchiolectasis and scarring is unchanged. There is no pleural
effusion.

The patient is status post left mastectomy.

Images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

There are no bone lesions in the thorax worrisome for infection or malignancy.

IMPRESSION: 

Interval improvement in bilateral upper lobe opacities since ___
suggests resolving fungal infection.

However, peripheral and basal predominant opacities now have a more
peribronchovascular and perilobular distribution, with associated bronchial
dilation and distortion.  This pattern is characteristic of organizing
pneumonia.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with COP s.p allotransplant. Need urgent CT chest
as pt has worsening SOB.  // ___ year old man with COP s.p allotransplant. Need
urgent CT chest as pt has worsening SOB.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 308.0 mGy cm

COMPARISON:  Several prior chest CT examinations dating from ___
through ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is unremarkable.

Heart size is normal without significant pericardial fluid. The thoracic
aortic arch and main pulmonary artery are normal in caliber. Scattered mildly
prominent mediastinal lymph nodes measure up to 8 mm in the short axis,
unchanged from prior study and on enlarged by CT size criteria. There is no
supraclavicular, axillary, hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy by CT size
criteria.

There is trace biapical scarring. There has been diffuse worsening of
bilateral lower zone predominant peripheral peribronchial and
peribronchovascular ground-glass opacities and areas of consolidation. There
is re- demonstration of diffuse bronchiectasis, surrounding scarring and
associated volume loss. Airways appear to remain patent to the subsegmental
level.

3 mm subpleural ground-glass nodule in the posterior segment of the right
upper lobe is stable (5:92). 2 mm nodule in the anterior segment of the right
upper lobe is unchanged (5:90). Another 2 mm nodule in the anterior segment of
the right upper lobe is stable (5:98). Punctate granuloma is present in the
lateral basal segment of the right lower lobe. Other scattered millimetric
nodules are unchanged. There is no new large consolidation. There is no
pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

While this study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, the imaged
upper abdomen is notable for diverticulosis.

Osseous structures and soft tissues: There is no suspicious focal osseous
lesion. There are mild multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
Re- demonstration of postsurgical change from left mastectomy.

IMPRESSION:


1. Mild interval progression of diffuse lower zone and peripheral predominant
peribronchial and peribronchovascular ground-glass opacities since ___.  Features remain suggestive of cryptogenic organizing pneumonia.
2. Stable areas of right middle lobe and lingular predominant scarring,
traction bronchiectasis and volume loss.
3. Stable scattered sub 4 mm nodules.
4. Diverticulosis.

AML with known pulmonary nodules  // f/u of pulmonary nodules

contrast and reconstructed as axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial

FINDINGS:

nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there is no appreciable coronary calcification. 
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.

Multiple pulmonary nodules are unchanged from ___.  For example:

A 2 mm nodule in the left upper lobe (series 4, image 40).

INDICATION:  ___ yo man with AML s/p allo transplant ___, now with likely
respiratory infection (cough/SOB). Need CT to eval pulmonary status/infection

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there is no appreciable coronary calcification.
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  Small hiatal hernia.

Mild diffuse bronchial wall thickening and irregularity has increased since
the prior examination.  Although an expiratory scan was not obtained, relative
mosaic attenuation of the lungs, most pronounced in the lower lobes is
suggestive of air trapping.

lower lobe and the lingula.

intra-abdominal organs appear grossly normal.

There are no bone findings of malignancy.

Patient is status post left mastectomy.  Mild right gynecomastia is unchanged.

1. Mild diffuse bronchial wall thickening has increased since the prior
examination.  This could potentially reflect on acute bronchitis in the
appropriate clinical setting, versus progressive chronic graft-versus-host
disease.

2. Numerous pulmonary nodules are stable in size and morphology.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:      AML

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS COORDINATED WITH
INTRAVENOUS INFUSION OF NONIONIC CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5
AND 1.25 MM THICK AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP
AXIAL IMAGES. SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS AND THE TOTAL
DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP reported separatelymGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

Small, subcentimeter axillary and mediastinal lymph nodes are similar in size
and number to the prior CT.  Heart size is normal, and there is no pericardial
effusion.  Small bilateral pleural effusions are new, dependent in location,
with adjacent bibasilar atelectasis.  Additional foci of linear atelectasis
are present in the mid lungs bilaterally.

Skeletal structures of the thorax demonstrate no new suspicious lytic or
blastic lesions.

Within the lungs, assessment is limited by inadvertent expiratory phase of
respiration and motion artifact.  Diffuse ground-glass opacities and septal
thickening are new since the prior CT. Previously reported subcentimeter
nodules are difficult to compare to the prior study due to the technical
limitations described above.  A 9 mm sub solid nodule in the left upper lobe
(97, 6) is potentially slightly more dense and larger than on the prior study,
but technical differences limit comparison.  Additionally, a few of the
previously described opacities are potentially obscured by new dependent
atelectasis.



IMPRESSION: 

1.  New ground-glass opacities with septal thickening and dependent small
pleural effusions, most suggestive of hydrostatic edema.  Differential
diagnosis includes atypical infection and less likely leukemic infiltration.

2. Pre-existing lung nodules are difficult to compare to the prior CT due to
technical limitations of today's exam.  Consider a ___ month followup CT to
allow more precise comparison of a potentially growing left upper lobe nodule
in order to exclude the possibility of a slowly growing lesion within the lung
adenocarcinoma spectrum.

3.  Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for complete
description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ man with AML s/p allogeneic transplant ___. 
Relapsed AML recently s/p DLI. Has had URI symptoms for last week, worsening
and history of pulmonary aspergillus and likely COP in past.  Evaluate for
infection.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:   Total DLP (Body) = 579 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.
CT abdomen pelvis dated ___.
CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

This exam is motion limited.  Within this limitation:

The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  There is an aberrant right
subclavian artery off the proximal descending thoracic aorta.  The main, left,
and right pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber without filling defect
indicate the presence of any acute incidental central pulmonary embolus. 
Heart is normal in configuration.  Trace pericardial fluid is likely
physiologic.

No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy by CT size
criteria.  Scattered mediastinal lymph nodes are measurable up to 6 mm in
short axis.

Bronchovascular micronodules in the lingula and upper lobe are new and most
consistent with infection (series 4, image 124, 113, 88).  No specific
evidence to suggest fungal infection.  Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules
are unchanged from the prior exam.  Specifically, a 4-mm right lower lobe
ground glass and solid nodule is unchanged since at least ___.

Secretions in the bronchus intermedius are minimal (series 4, image 124). 
Secretions within the trachea on the prior exam are gone.  There is probably a
small left mainstem bronchus diverticulum (series 4, image 120).  Diffuse
cylindrical bronchiectasis is mild.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

The imaged thyroid is unremarkable.

No osseous lesions in the chest cage concerning for malignancy or infection. 
Multi-level degenerative changes in the thoracic spine are mild.  No acute
fracture.  There is asymmetry of the breast, suggesting prior excision on the
left.  There is right asymmetric gynecomastia.

This exam is not dedicated for imaging of the upper abdomen, but the partially
imaged upper abdomen is unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

New left upper lobe peribronchovascular micronodules suggesting atypical
infection, such as viral.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 3:32 ___, 15 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with hypoxia, dyspnea, AML// ?PE or pneumonia

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 403 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus.
Right PICC line terminates in the low SVC.  Incidental note is made of an
aberrant retroesophageal course of the right subclavian artery.  Minimal
atherosclerotic calcifications at the aortic arch.  The aorta is normal in
caliber.  No aortic dissection is seen.  No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.  The esophagus is quite
distended with fluid, from the stomach to the upper chest.  No esophageal wall
thickening.

PLEURAL SPACES: Small bilateral nonhemorrhagic pleural effusions are new since
___.  No pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Peribronchial nodular opacities within the right upper lobe are
similar compared to ___, likely infectious or inflammatory in
nature.  Compressive atelectasis within the lower lobes bilaterally due to the
pleural effusions.  Linear atelectasis within the lingula.  No new focal
consolidations.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.
The thyroid gland is homogeneous.

ABDOMEN: The partially stomach is extremely distended with fluid and debris. 
No additional abnormalities within the partially visualized upper abdomen.

BONES:  There is no acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic dissection.
2. Peribronchial nodular opacities within the upper lobe are similar compared
to ___, likely infectious or inflammatory nature.
3. New small bilateral pleural effusions with associated atelectasis.
4. The esophagus and partially imaged stomach are extremely distended with
fluid.  Esophagus is quite distended with fluid/debris from the stomach to the
upper chest.  Query slow transit from the stomach.
This also places patient at increased risk for aspiration.
